title: atriplex angustifolia subsp. erecta
intro: Atriplex angustifolia subsp. erecta is a subspecies of the Atriplex angustifolia plant, commonly known as four-wing saltbush. It is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Atriplex angustifolia subsp. erecta can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
